reasoning: The Lean formalization captures the informal claim precisely. The local `IsRegularLocalRing` class correctly encodes "regular local ring" via Mathlib's `CotangentSpace` (= m/m²) equipped with `ResidueField R`-module structure, and equates its `finrank` to `ringKrullDim R`, which is the standard definition. The hypothesis `[P.LiesOver (maximalIdeal R)]` unfolds (via `Ideal.LiesOver.over`) to `maximalIdeal R = P.comap (algebraMap R R[X])`, i.e., m = P ∩ R. The conclusion `IsRegularLocalRing (Localization.AtPrime P)` correctly formalizes "R[x]_P is regular". No hypothesis is silently dropped or added beyond what the informal text implies.
